violations.text: Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

French fries were observed held at 112-119F in hot-hot unit. Per interview with PIC, fries get reheated before service. Inspector informed PIC that food must be held at 135F or above. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025
Walk-in cooler located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, inspector observed dripping condensate. Per interview with PIC, work order has been submitted. New Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2025

The vents/ceiling tiles in the kitchen area has an accumulation of dust, debris and soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2025

Lettuce and tomatoes were observed held at 45-55 degrees Fahrenheit in the ice well. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the ice well for less than 4 hours. Food was relocated to cool during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

Chicken Caesar salad and cheese were observed held at 45-47 degrees Fahrenheit in the grab & go cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the prep unit for less than 4 hours. Food was relocated to cool. The ambient air temperature was observed at 50 degrees Fahrenheit during time of visit. Per person in charge, grab & go cooler is turned off at the last lunch. Establishment must keep refrigeration on at all times when cold holding and must not use the unit if it cannot maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

Cheese was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/10/2023
